_meta.title: GodsEye Facilities Intelligence
_meta.note: Dossiers combine public-record technical data with GodsEye canon (strikes absorbed, current status). Public figures are pre-conflict baselines unless dated. Conflict status from GodsEye chronology.
_meta.sources: Offshore-technology · TotalEnergies · Wood Mackenzie · POGC · EIA · GodsEye chronology v5

facilities.name: South Pars — Offshore Field
facilities.operator: Pars Oil & Gas Company (POGC), subsidiary of NIOC
facilities.runs_to: Onshore processing trains at Asaluyeh, then Iranian domestic grid + IGAT pipelines
facilities.capacity: ~730 mcm/day gas (record, Jan–Feb 2026); ~1m bbl/day condensate at full phases
facilities.reserves: ~14 tcm gas + ~18bn bbl condensate (Iranian side). Shared with Qatar's North Field; >2,000 tcf combined — world's largest gas accumulation, ~10% of global reserves
facilities.revenue: Feeds ~two-thirds of Iran's gas supply; domestic-market oriented since Total's 2018 exit
facilities.issues: Reservoir pressure fallen ~5,200→3,500 PSI over 20 yrs. $17bn pressure-preservation upgrade reported (S&P, Mar 2025) to arrest decline. 308 of 341 wells active on 38 platforms.
facilities.strikes: NOT STRUCK. The offshore reservoir and platforms are undamaged. This is load-bearing in GodsEye canon: the field produces; the Iranian onshore processing is what failed.
facilities.godseye: Distinguish always from the Asaluyeh onshore trains. FIRMS thermal on the Qatari side (Ras Laffan) runs +47% while the Iranian onshore runs −31% — same reservoir, opposite thermal trend. Wrapper thesis.

facilities.name: Asaluyeh — South Pars Onshore Trains
facilities.operator: POGC / NIOC; Pars Special Economic Energy Zone
facilities.runs_to: IGAT trunk pipelines to Iranian grid; export line to Turkey (BOTAŞ)
facilities.built: Trains commissioned progressively 2004–2023 alongside offshore phases
facilities.cost: Onshore portion of each ~$1–1.5bn phase; refinery complexes additional
facilities.capacity: Processes the field's ~730 mcm/day at full running; condensate stabilisation, LPG, ethane, sulfur
facilities.revenue: Gateway for effectively all South Pars gas into the Iranian economy
facilities.issues: The single point of failure between a healthy offshore field and the domestic grid. Onshore trains are soft, unhardened, and irreplaceable at speed.
facilities.strikes: STRUCK. Onshore processing trains hit during the conflict. Iranian gas flow to Turkey stopped following the strike (Bloomberg). GodsEye FIRMS: thermal −31% vs pre-conflict.
facilities.godseye: The struck node in the South Pars wrapper thesis. Offshore = fine, onshore = failed. Bilateral proof: Qatar's Ras Laffan (same reservoir) steady/rising while Asaluyeh falls.

facilities.name: Ras Laffan — Qatar (North Field)
facilities.runs_to: LNG export to Asia, Europe, Africa; North Field South expansion ongoing
facilities.built: Industrial city developed from 1996; continuous LNG train expansion
facilities.cost: Tens of $bn cumulative; North Field expansion a further major programme
facilities.capacity: One of the world's largest LNG complexes; secures Qatar's top-tier LNG exporter status through 2026
facilities.strikes: NOT STRUCK. Qatari side of the shared reservoir unaffected.
facilities.godseye: The perfect control for the South Pars wrapper thesis: same rock, different owner. FIRMS +47% here while Iranian onshore −31% proves the reservoir is not the variable — the Iranian plant is.

facilities.name: Kharg Island Terminal
facilities.runs_to: ~90% of Iranian crude exports load here; T-jetty (east) + sea-island (west); tankers to Asia via Hormuz
facilities.built: Terminal operational since 1960s; repeatedly rebuilt after Tanker War damage
facilities.cost: State strategic asset; not publicly costed
facilities.capacity: Historic ~90% of national crude export throughput; ~5-mile island, 7,000 technical staff, 8,000–20,000 residents
facilities.revenue: The single most important revenue chokepoint in the Iranian economy
facilities.issues: Fortified in 2026: MANPADS, shoreline mines, anti-armour emplacements. Cannot be defended against standoff attack (proven in the 1980s Tanker War).
facilities.strikes: Multiple. May 6 2026 fire + slick event (GodsEye original analysis). M/T Belma disabled by US fire at the export gate under blockade #2. Named as a US seizure-option target 14 Jul (Situation Room). Berth occupancy and thermal both flat under blockade — no measurable export squeeze despite the pressure.
facilities.godseye: The named prize of the ground-campaign option. ~20,000-person population problem no published plan addresses. FIRMS 39% below same-week 2025; throttled, not burning.

facilities.name: Bandar Abbas
facilities.operator: IRGC Navy / Iranian Navy; commercial port by Shahid Rajaee
facilities.runs_to: Primary Iranian naval base on the strait; commercial container gateway; Shahid Rajaee refinery nearby
facilities.built: Long-established; Shahid Rajaee port a modern container complex
facilities.capacity: Main IRGC/Navy south coast base; the KIAN shuttle to Karachi originates here
facilities.strikes: STRUCK. 12 Jul 2026: first combat use of US unmanned surface vessels in history — three Saronic Corsair kamikaze USVs hit the sub/ship maintenance facility, striking a Ghadir-class midget submarine on its gantry. A bridge near Bandar Abbas severely damaged in later waves (AP).
facilities.godseye: Land-transport interdiction node. Convoy counts in this box may distort from bridge damage — read before treating movers as pure logistics tempo.

facilities.name: Chabahar / Konarak
facilities.operator: IRGC Navy (Imam Ali Base); Chabahar commercial port (India-operated concession)
facilities.runs_to: Makran-coast dhow route — origin of weapons/materiel flow to Yemen and the Horn of Africa
facilities.built: Chabahar port developed with Indian investment as a Gulf-bypass trade route
facilities.cost: Indian concession investment in commercial port
facilities.capacity: IRGC south-east naval complex; the actual Yemen resupply origin
facilities.issues: Only Iranian ocean port outside the Gulf — bypasses Hormuz entirely.
facilities.strikes: STRUCK. 21 Jul 2026: heavy US strikes on Chabahar and Konarak including the IRGC's Imam Ali Base (Haalvsh, with video). A strike on the Yemen supply line at its source — not a Hormuz-shipping target, whatever the CENTCOM framing says.
facilities.godseye: Divergence between stated purpose (freedom of navigation) and revealed target set (Houthi resupply chain).

facilities.name: Fujairah
facilities.operator: Port of Fujairah / UAE; ADNOC ADCOP pipeline terminus
facilities.runs_to: World's 2nd-largest bunkering hub; ADCOP pipeline (~1.5 mbd) delivers Abu Dhabi crude here, bypassing Hormuz
facilities.built: Bunkering hub developed late 20th c.; ADCOP online 2012
facilities.capacity: Major anchorage + the only significant Hormuz bypass loading point outside the threat corridor
facilities.revenue: Bunker fuel + Abu Dhabi crude export
facilities.issues: Inside the Gulf of Oman threat envelope.
facilities.strikes: Not struck. But near Fujairah the Hui Chuan armoury was seized by IRGC 14 May (NE of Fujairah).
facilities.godseye: Lost ~250 hulls (−19%) as traffic fled the Hormuz envelope. Shadow share rose 10.6→17.0%. The displacement origin — tonnage moved from here to Karachi, Jamnagar, Duqm.

facilities.name: Yanbu
facilities.runs_to: Red Sea export terminus of the East–West (Petroline) pipeline; the Hormuz bypass
facilities.capacity: Petroline ~7 mbd; ~1 mbd consumed by 3 west-coast refineries; net ~5 mbd export. Ran ~4.7 mbd immediately pre-blockade
facilities.issues: Delivers into the Red Sea — the sea the Houthi blockade now threatens. The bypass became a single point of failure.
facilities.strikes: Not struck. But loadings −36% after the 20 Jul Houthi declaration; two VLCCs (VL Pioneer, Farhah) U-turned 17 Jul.
facilities.godseye: The measured proof of the two-chokepoint squeeze: 4 mbd here (vs 973k bpd a year ago) now sits inside the declared blockade zone.

facilities.name: Ras Tanura
facilities.runs_to: Largest Saudi crude export terminal; Gulf/east-coast loading → Hormuz
facilities.built: Operational since 1939; world's largest oil terminal complex
facilities.issues: Hormuz-locked — everything loaded here must transit the strait.
facilities.godseye: Why the Saudi pivot to Yanbu mattered: with Hormuz interdicted, east-coast loading is exposed, forcing the west-coast reroute that Bab el-Mandeb then trapped.

facilities.name: Shinas / Sohar (Oman)
facilities.runs_to: Omani discharge/anchorage on the Gulf of Oman, outside the strait
facilities.issues: Highest shadow-flag concentration in the theatre.
facilities.godseye: 19.7% shadow share — highest of any box measured, and unchanged from 2025 (was already the shadow port). OFAC-sanctioned VLCC ATILA currently declaring Shinas. Watch as the Omani-side discharge point.

facilities.name: Pickaxe Mountain (Kuh-e Kolang Gaz La)
facilities.runs_to: Assessed centrifuge / enrichment tunnel complex, ~1 mile S of Natanz
facilities.built: Continuous construction ~15 months (ISIS); replaces the centrifuge-assembly plant destroyed in the 2020 sabotage
facilities.capacity: 90–140 m (295–460 ft) beneath rock — deeper than Fordow. Iran holds ~450 kg of 60%-enriched uranium.
facilities.issues: Declared-intent, access-denied (Iran notified IAEA 2021, denied inspection). Plausibly beyond GBU-57 MOP penetration. Requires B-2 / Diego Garcia, not B-1.
facilities.strikes: NOT STRUCK. Pre-announced twice by Trump (13 & 21 Jul) and unstruck through the heaviest strike tempo of the war. Under TACO, the un-struck mountain is evidence for the reverse-indicator read.
facilities.godseye: Israeli assessment (WSJ) that centrifuges were moved here after the June 2025 strikes. If correct, the strikes PRODUCED this — the inversion: kinetic action against an unverified programme buries it further from verification.

facilities.name: Muwaffaq Salti Air Base (Azraq)
facilities.operator: Royal Jordanian Air Force; hosts US forces
facilities.runs_to: US air operations; struck by Iranian ballistic missiles 17 Jul
facilities.issues: US forces here administratively assigned to Operation Inherent Resolve (anti-ISIS), not the Iran operation — an authorisation seam.
facilities.strikes: STRUCK by Iran. 17 Jul 2026: 2 US KIA (Pvt Isabella Gonzales, 1st Lt Tyler Feehan — both air-defence artillery), 1 initially MIA, 4 medevaced. First official US fatalities of the exchange.
facilities.godseye: The base whose function is defence, penetrated. FT: Iranian tracking via telecom + commercial location data explains the accuracy.

facilities.name: Hui Chuan — seizure point
facilities.operator: Sinoguards Marine Security (Hong Kong); SG Navigation (Marshall Is.)
facilities.runs_to: Floating armoury — embarks/recovers armed guard teams for Hormuz transits
facilities.issues: Mixed UK/China/Ukraine/Nepal crew — no Chinese-national bloc, by design.
facilities.strikes: SEIZED by IRGC 14 May 2026, ~38nm NE of Fujairah, escorted into Iranian waters as a 'documentation and compliance inspection'.
facilities.godseye: The template. Every layer — Chinese company not state vessel, Honduras flag, Marshall Is. operator, mixed crew, 'inspection' not seizure — engineered so no Chinese obligation to respond is generated. Earliest instance of the threshold-management architecture; message to Beijing on the day of the Trump summit.

facilities.name: Sunny Ocean — on station
facilities.operator: Sinoguards Marine Security; sister to Hui Chuan
facilities.runs_to: Floating armoury holding the southern Red Sea transit lane
facilities.issues: Position identical to 2 decimals for 62 days — station-keeping (variable reception hours favour genuine presence over static broadcast).
facilities.strikes: None. Still on station, still broadcasting as of 21 Jul.
facilities.godseye: Parked on the transit lane since 16 May and did NOT move when the Houthi blockade was declared 20 Jul. The inaction is the signal — Sinoguards positioned for armed transits to continue, not for the corridor to close. Commercial money betting against the headline.
